records.sceneDescription: G21:1-7 Birth of Isaac
G21:8-9 Ishmael mocks Isaac
G21:10-13 Sarah asking Abraham to drive out Hagar
G21:14-19 Hagar being driven out
G21:20-21 Hagar and Ishmael settles down
G21:22-32 Abraham’s covenant with Abimelech (King of Gerar)
G21:33-34 Abraham plants a tamarisk tree at Beersheba
G22:1-8 Jehovah puts Abraham to the test
G22:9-13 Jehovah’s angel stops Abraham from sacrificing Isaac
G22:15-19 Jehovah blessing Abraham
G22:20-24 Rebekah’s family
G23:1-2 Abraham mourns the death of Sarah
G23:3–20 Abraham buys the cave at Machpelah to bury Sarah
G24:1-11 Abraham instructs his servant and sends him to Mesopotamia
G24:12-14 Eliezer’s prayer to Jehovah
G24:15-27 Abraham’s servant meets Rebekah and her family
G24:28-33 Rebekah relates meeting the stranger at the well to her family
G24:34-61 Abraham’s servant returning with Rebekah from Mesopotamia
G24:62-67 Isaac meets Rebekah
G25:1-6 Abraham takes Keturah as wife
G25:7-11 Death of Abraham
G25:12-16 History of Ishmael after the death of Abraham
G25:17-18 Death of Ishmael
G25:19-26 Twin sons were born to Isaac and Rebekah
G25:27-34 Esau sells his birthright to Jacob
G26:1-11 During the famine Isaac goes to Abimelech, in Gerar
G26:12-22 Isaac in Gerar; Jehovah blessing him abundantly
G26:23-25 Jehovah appearing to Isaac in Beersheba
G26:26-33 Abimelech establishes a covenant with Isaac
G26:34-35 Esau takes two Hittite wives
G27:1-27 Rebekah sends Jacob to receive blessings from Isaac
G27:28-29 Jacob gets the blessing from Isaac
G27:30-39 Esau prepares a dish for Isaac to receive his blessing
G27:39-40 Isaac blesses Esau
G27:41-45 Esau plots to murder his brother Jacob
G27:46 Rebekah is disgusted with the wives of Esau
G28:1-5 Isaac blesses Jacob and sends him away to Laban
G28:6-9 Esau marries again to please Isaac, his father
G28:10-22 Jacob departs for Haran; Jacob’s dream
G29:1-12 Jacob meets Rachel at the well
G29:13-24 Laban tricks Jacob in to marrying Leah
G29:25-29 Jacob marries Rachel
G29:30-35 Four sons are born to Jacob through Leah
G30:1-8 Rachel gives Bilhah as wife to Jacob
G30:9-13 Leah gives Zilpah as wife to Jacob
G30:14-21 Leah gives her son’s mandrakes to Rachel in exchange for Jacob
G30:22-25 Rachel has a son
G30:25-43 Jacob stays with Laban for another 6 years for the flocks
G31:1-3 Jehovah instructs Jacob to return
G31:4-16 Jacob sent messages to  Leah and Rachel to meet him at the field
G31:17-21 Jacob runs away from Laban
G31:22-24 Jehovah appears to Laban
G31:25-42 Laban catches up with Jacob
G31:43-55 Jacob and Laban establish a covenant
G32:1-2 Angels of God meet up with Jacob
G32:3-6 Jacob sends messengers to Esau
G32:7-8 Jacob divides the camp in to two
G32:9-12 Jacob prays to Jehovah to save him from the hand of Esau
G32:13-21 Jacob sends gifts from his possessions to Esau
G32:22-23 Jacob takes his family and crosses Jordan at the ford of Jabbok
G32:24-32 Jacob wrestles with an angel
G33:1-11 Esau meets Jacob and his family
G33:12-17 Jacob builds a house in Succoth
G33:18-20 Jacob moves to Shechem
G34:1-12 Dinah is violated by Shechem
G34:13-24 Jacob’s sons tricks Hamor, Shechem and the men of the city
G34:25-29 Simeon and Levi kill every man in the city of Shechem
G34:30-31 Jacob fears backlash for killing the men of Shechem
G35:1-4 Jehovah instructs Jacob to dwell in Bethel
G35:5-7 Jacob builds an altar in Bethel
G35:8 Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse dies
G35:9-13 Jehovah appears to Jacob
G35:16-20 Rachel dies giving birth to her second son
G35:21-22 Reuben lies down with Bilhah, Jacob’s concubine
G35:22b-26 Listing of all 12 sons of Jacob
G35:27 Jacob returns to his father at Mamre
G35:28-29 Death of Isaac
G36:1-5 History of Esau
G36:6-8 Esau leaves Canaan
G36:9-19 History of Esau, the father of Edom
G36:20-30 The sons of Seir the Horite
G36:31-39 The Kings of Edom
G36:40-43 Sheikhs of Esau
G37:1-4 Jacob loved Joseph more than all his brothers
G37:5-11 Jospeh’s dreams
G37:12-17 Israel sends Joseph to his brothers
G37:18-24 Joseph’s brothers plot to kill him
G37:25-28 Joseph is sold to Ishmaelites (Midianite merchants) for 20 pieces of silver
G37:29-30 Reuben rips his garment when he cannot find Joseph in the pit
G37:31-35 Jacob mourns the death of his son after seeing the blood stained robe
G37:36 Midianites sell Joseph in Egypt to Potipher
G38:1-5 Judah moves to Adullam and marries the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite woman
G38:6-10 Jehovah puts two of Judah’s sons to death
G38:11 Judah requests Tamar to stay at her father’s house
G38:12-19 Tamar tricks Judah in to having relations with her, and keeps his belongings
G38:20-26 Tamar becomes pregnant, and Judah acknowledges his mistake
G38:27-30 Two sons are born to Judah through Tamar
G39:1-6 Egyptian court official named Potiphar buys Joseph
G39:7-20 Joseph is false accused of trying to lie down with Potiphar’s wife and he is put in jail
G39:21-23 Jehovah blesses Joseph; He was made in charge of all the prisoners
G40:1-19 Joseph interprets two dreams
G40:20-23 Joseph’s interpretation of the dreams come true
G41:1-7 Pharaoh’s dreams
G41:8-13 Chief cupbearer mentions about Joseph to Pharaoh
G41:14-16 Joseph is called from the prison to interpret the dreams
G41:17-24 Pharaoh relates his dreams to Joseph
G41:25-36 Joseph interprets Pharaoh's dreams
G41:37-46a Joseph is given authority over the land of Egypt
G41:46b-49 Joseph stockpile grains during the 7 years of abundance
G41:50-52 Two sons are born to Joseph through Asenath
G41:53-57 All the land of Egypt suffers from a famine for 7 years, as foretold
G42:1-4 Jacob instructs his sons go down to Egypt and buy grain
G42:5-7 Israel’s sons reach Egypt, to Joseph, to buy grains
G42:8-17 Joseph accuses his brothers of being spies
G42:18-25 Joseph demands one of Israel’s sons to stay back and return to Egypt with Benjamin
G42:26-28 One of them find the money on the bag when he opens it
G42:29-38 Jacob refuses to send Benjamin to Egypt
G43:1-7 Israel urges his sons to go back to get more grains, but is reluctant to send Benjamin
G43:8-14 Judah promises Israel to take responsibility for Benjamin’s safety
G43:15-34 Joseph orders his servants to take his brothers home and prepare a meal for them
G44:1-2 Joseph instructs his servant to leave his silver cup in Benjamin’s bag
G44:3-17 The silver cup is found in Benjamin’s bag and they all appear before Joseph
G44:18-34 Judah expresses his plight to Joseph that he cannot return to his father without Benjamin
G45:1-8 Joseph reveals that he is their brother whom they sold
G45:9-15 Joseph instructs his brothers to go back and bring his father and all his household to Egypt
G45:16-20 News reaches Pharaoh about Joseph’s family and he invites them all to Egypt
G45:21-28 Joseph’s brothers come back to Canaan to their father and tells him Joseph is alive
G46:1-4 Israel departs for Egypt, offers sacrifice to Jehovah in Beersheba
G46:5-7 Israel departs Beersheba and comes in to Egypt
G46:8-27 Names of Israel’s sons who came in to Egypt
G46:28-30 Joseph meets his father Israel in Goshen
G46:31-47:10 Joseph presents his brothers and his father Israel to Pharaoh
G47:11-12 Joseph settles his father and his household in Goshen
G47:13-26 Joseph takes the money, livestock, and land from the people of Egypt for Pharaoh
G47:27-31 Israel is nearing end of his life
G48:1-22 Israel blesses Joseph
G49:1-2 Israel’s death bed prophecy
G49:3-4 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Reuben
G49:5-7 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Simeon and Levi
G49:8-12 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Judah
G49:13 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Zebulun
G49:14-15 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Issachar
G49:16-18 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Dan
G49:19-21 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Gad, Asher, Naphtali
G49:22-26 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Joseph
G49:27 Israel’s death bed prophecy: Benjamin
G49:28-33 Death of Israel
G50:1-3 Joseph commands to embalm his father
G50:4-6 Joseph asks Pharaoh to let him bury his father in Canaan
G50:7-14 Joseph and his brothers go up to Canaan to bury their father
G50:15-21 Fearing for their lives, Joseph’s brothers send him a message
